content.text: One thing we haven't discussed from an architecture perspective is an admin console. We don't need anything crazy fancy at the start, but several things stand out as being necessary for a v1 product: 1) pre-appproval of email addresses as a prerequisite for account creation (or required post-creation approval by an admin) because we don't necessarily want anyone on the intranet to be able to create and account and provision resources; 2) change user quotas, or possibly switch between two levels of non-admin accounts: `standard` and a power user, which would allow us to easily provide/modify group-level permissions and/or quotas; 3) manage or initiate resetting of user passwords; 4) view and manage all jobs across all users (include jobs that have been "deleted" by users, which shouldn't actually delete the job but should instead just remove the job from the user's job history); etc. Any other obvious admin-level tasks that the v1 console should have?

Good catch — the admin console is a genuine v1 gap, and it has real data-model implications we should settle before it goes into `ARCHITECTURE.md`. Let me validate your four, propose the additions I think are clearly v1, then surface the couple of real forks.

## Your four — confirmed, with the shape each implies

1. **Account gating** → a `User.status` (`pending`/`active`/`disabled`) and/or an email allowlist/invite table. *(fork below — which mechanism)*
2. **Quotas / tiers** → I'd split `role` (admin vs. not) from **`tier`** (`standard`/`power`), where a tier carries a default quota profile, **plus** per-user override fields. That gives you both group-level *and* individual control — strictly more capable than either alone and barely more work, so I'd just do both rather than choose. *(confirm)*
3. **Password resets** → a reset-token mechanism; admin can initiate. *(fork below — delivery)*
4. **Job oversight + soft-delete** → `Run.hidden_at` so a user "delete" only hides the run from their history; admin sees everything including hidden runs, and can cancel/kill running jobs and **hard-delete to reclaim storage**.

## Additional v1 capabilities I'd recommend

Each reuses the service layer and existing data — no new infrastructure:

- **System / GPU status dashboard** — the 8 cards (free/busy + what's running on each), queue depth and waits. Essential operational visibility for a shared node; it's how an admin answers "why is my job waiting?"
- **Usage reporting** — per-user and system-wide GPU-hours over time. You're already recording GPU-hours; surfacing them *directly* serves the "usage tracking / fair shared resource" pillar and informs quota decisions.
- **Suspend/disable a user** — block access while retaining data (offboarding, abuse); reuses the same `status` field as gating.
- **Admin role management + first-admin bootstrap** — grant/revoke admin, and seed the first admin via env/CLI on deploy (non-optional: with gating, nobody can self-approve into existence).
- **Maintenance mode** — pause job dispatch (let running jobs finish, hold the queue) for node maintenance or updating autobio images. Cheap and genuinely useful on a single node.
- **Tool catalog management** — trigger the autobio→catalog sync and enable/disable individual tools (e.g. hide a broken one).
- **Lightweight admin audit log** — who approved/suspended/changed-quota/reset/cancelled. Cheap accountability for a multi-admin resource — but the easiest thing to defer if you want v1 leaner.

**Defer past v1:** storage retention/auto-cleanup policies, permissions finer than the three levels, and richer dashboards.

**One architectural note:** the admin console isn't a separate system — it's admin-scoped API endpoints + SPA admin views, all going through the **same service layer** (so quota edits, cancellations, etc. reuse the existing domain logic). This is the bespoke admin UI we knowingly accepted when we chose FastAPI over Django's free admin; it stays cheap precisely because it reuses the core.

Now the two real forks:

questions.question: How should account creation be gated?

options.label: Email allowlist / invite (pre-approval)
options.description: Admin pre-approves emails (or sends invites); only approved addresses can register. Gates at the door — no account or resources provisioned before approval. Recommended.

options.label: Post-creation admin approval
options.description: Anyone on the intranet can register, but the account sits `pending` and can't submit jobs until an admin activates it. Catches self-serve users, but you manage a pending queue.

options.label: Both
options.description: Allowlist controls who can register, and admins retain activate/suspend control afterward. Most flexible, slightly more surface area.

questions.question: How should password resets work in v1? (This hinges on whether the intranet has a usable SMTP/mail server.)

options.label: Admin-mediated (no email dependency)
options.description: Admin initiates a reset; the system generates a one-time link or temporary password the admin shares out-of-band, and the user sets a new one. Works even with no mail server. Recommended for v1.

options.label: Self-service via email (requires SMTP)
options.description: User requests a reset and gets an emailed link. Better UX, but depends on a working intranet mail server.

options.label: Both
options.description: Self-service email reset for users, with admin-initiated reset as a fallback. Best UX, but needs SMTP and a bit more to build.

result.output: Your questions have been answered: "How should account creation be gated?"="Both", "How should password resets work in v1? (This hinges on whether the intranet has a usable SMTP/mail server.)"="Admin-mediated (no email dependency)". You can now continue with these answers in mind.

content.text:
Locked in:

- **Gating: Both** — allowlist/invite controls who can register, *and* new accounts sit `pending` until an admin activates them (with `disabled` for later suspension).
- **Password reset: admin-mediated** — one-time token/temp-password shared out-of-band, no SMTP dependency.
- **Quotas: tier profile (`standard`/`power`) + per-user override** (confirmed).
- Plus the additional v1 capabilities I proposed, including a lightweight audit log (the easy thing to drop later if you want).

Since PR #2 is still open and unmerged, the admin console belongs *in* the architecture doc — so I'll fold all of this into `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` on the same `docs/architecture` branch. Making the edits now.

input.old_string:
- **User** — identity (from the auth provider), display name, role
  (`user` / `admin`), quota config. Everything scopes to a user.
input.new_string:
- **User** — identity (from the auth provider), display name, and three
  orthogonal fields the admin console manages:
  - **`role`** — `user` / `admin` (admin gates access to the admin console).
  - **`tier`** — `standard` / `power`; maps to a default quota profile
    (config-defined), so quotas can be managed at the group level.
  - **`status`** — `pending` / `active` / `disabled`. New accounts start
    `pending` until an admin activates them; `disabled` blocks access while
    retaining all data.
  Plus optional **per-user quota overrides** that take precedence over the tier
  profile. Everything scopes to a user.

info, output-dir pointer, and a soft-delete marker (`hidden_at`) — a user
  "delete" only sets `hidden_at` (removing the run from their history); the
  record and outputs are retained and remain visible to admins.

input.old_string:
- **Artifact** — files a run produced: name, type, size, path. A lightweight
  index over the run's output directory, populated on completion. The bytes live
  on disk; the DB only points at them.
input.new_string:
- **Artifact** — files a run produced: name, type, size, path. A lightweight
  index over the run's output directory, populated on completion. The bytes live
  on disk; the DB only points at them.
- **AllowedEmail / Invitation** — the registration allowlist: approved email
  addresses (optionally carrying an invite token), with who added them and when.
  Registration is refused for any address not on the list.
- **PasswordResetToken** — one-time, expiring tokens minted when an admin
  initiates a reset; the user exchanges the token (delivered out-of-band) for a
  new password.
- **AuditLog** — admin actions (approve/suspend a user, change a quota or tier,
  initiate a reset, cancel or hard-delete a run): actor, action, target,
  timestamp, details. Lightweight accountability for a multi-admin resource.
- **SystemSettings** — a small singleton of operational flags, notably
  `maintenance_mode` (dispatch paused).

input.old_string:
**Usage and quotas.** Usage (GPU-hours) is recorded on every run from day one,
for visibility and so budgets can be enabled later without backfilling. v1
**enforces a per-user concurrency cap** (max simultaneous queued/running runs) —
the control that actually prevents one user monopolizing the 8 GPUs. A GPU-hours
budget is a later, additive toggle.
input.new_string:
**Usage and quotas.** Usage (GPU-hours) is recorded on every run from day one,
for visibility and so budgets can be enabled later without backfilling. v1
**enforces a per-user concurrency cap** (max simultaneous queued/running runs) —
the control that actually prevents one user monopolizing the 8 GPUs. The cap
comes from the user's **tier** quota profile (`standard` / `power`) with an
optional **per-user override**, both set from the admin console. A GPU-hours
budget is a later, additive toggle over the same machinery.

input.old_string:
3. **Schedule.** The scheduler claims a `queued` run via
   `SELECT … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` once enough GPUs are free, marks it
   `running`, and assigns specific GPU IDs exclusively (count from the tool's
   metadata, default 1).
input.new_string:
3. **Schedule.** The scheduler claims a `queued` run via
   `SELECT … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` once enough GPUs are free, marks it
   `running`, and assigns specific GPU IDs exclusively (count from the tool's
   metadata, default 1). While `maintenance_mode` is set the scheduler stops
   claiming new runs and lets in-flight runs finish.

  **identity-provider boundary** so the mechanism is swappable.
input.new_string:
- **Mechanism (v1):** local accounts (app-managed users + passwords), behind an
  **identity-provider boundary** so the mechanism is swappable.
- **Registration is gated:** an email must be on the **allowlist** to register,
  and a new account stays **`pending`** until an admin activates it (see
  [Admin console](#admin-console)). The first admin is seeded out-of-band
  (env var / CLI) on deploy, since with gating no one can self-approve into
  existence.

content.text: Now the new Admin console section (inserted before the swappable boundaries) and the expanded non-goals:

input.new_string:
## Admin console

The admin console is **not a separate system** — it is a set of admin-scoped API
endpoints plus admin views in the same SPA, all routed through the **same service
layer** so every admin action (activating a user, changing a quota, cancelling a
run) reuses the existing domain logic rather than poking the database directly.
Access is gated by `role = admin`. This is the bespoke admin UI we accepted when
choosing FastAPI over Django's built-in admin; it stays cheap because it reuses
the core.

**v1 capabilities:**

- **User management**
  - Maintain the registration **allowlist** / send invitations.
  - **Activate** `pending` accounts and **suspend / re-enable** (`disabled`)
    users.
  - Set a user's **tier** (`standard` / `power`) and **per-user quota
    overrides**.
  - **Initiate password resets** (mints a one-time token shared out-of-band).
  - **Grant / revoke admin** `role`. The first admin is seeded via env/CLI on
    deploy.
- **Job oversight** — view **all** runs across users (including `hidden_at`
  runs), filter by user/status, **cancel/kill** running runs, and
  **hard-delete** runs to reclaim storage. (A user "delete" only hides; only an
  admin removes.)
- **System status** — live view of the 8-GPU pool (free/busy and what is running
  on each), plus queue depth and waits.
- **Usage reporting** — per-user and system-wide GPU-hours over time, to inform
  quota decisions and serve the "usage tracking / fair shared resource" goal.
- **Tool catalog management** — trigger the autobio → catalog sync and
  enable/disable individual tools.
- **Maintenance mode** — pause job dispatch (running jobs finish, the queue
  holds) for node maintenance or autobio image updates.
- **Audit log** — record admin actions for accountability.

## The swappable boundaries

result.output: The file /home/briney/git/fold-at-scripps/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.old_string:
- Hardened, security-critical auth.
- VRAM packing / GPU sharing.
- Server-sent-events / push status (polling is sufficient for v1).
input.new_string:
- Hardened, security-critical auth.
- VRAM packing / GPU sharing.
- Server-sent-events / push status (polling is sufficient for v1).
- Storage retention / automated cleanup policies (admins reclaim space manually).
- Permission granularity beyond `standard` / `power` / `admin`.
- Email-based / self-service account and password flows (admin-mediated in v1).

content.text:
Done — the admin console is now folded into `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` on the `docs/architecture` branch (so it extends the open PR #2 rather than fragmenting the architecture across PRs). Changes:

- **Data model — `User`** now carries `role` (admin access), `tier` (`standard`/`power` → quota profile), `status` (`pending`/`active`/`disabled`), and per-user quota overrides.
- **Data model — `Run`** gains `hidden_at` (user "delete" = hide only; record + outputs retained).
- **New entities:** `AllowedEmail/Invitation`, `PasswordResetToken`, `AuditLog`, `SystemSettings` (holds `maintenance_mode`).
- **Quotas** paragraph updated for tier-profile + per-user override.
- **Job lifecycle** step 3 notes the scheduler honors `maintenance_mode`.
- **Authentication** notes gating (allowlist + `pending` activation) and first-admin bootstrap, linking to the new section.
- **New "Admin console" section** covering user management, job oversight, system/GPU status, usage reporting, tool-catalog management, maintenance mode, and the audit log — explicitly framed as admin endpoints + SPA views over the same service layer.
- **Non-goals** expanded: storage retention/auto-cleanup, finer permissions, and self-service email/password flows.

Self-reviewed for consistency: no placeholders, the `#admin-console` cross-link resolves, and nothing contradicts the earlier sections.

Two things:

1. **Review the changes** — want anything trimmed (e.g. drop the audit log to keep v1 leaner) or expanded?
2. **Commit?** I'd push this to `docs/architecture`, which updates PR #2 in place. I can also refresh the PR description to mention the admin console. Say the word and I'll commit + push (and update the PR body).

input.command: git branch --show-current && git add docs/ARCHITECTURE.md && git commit -m "Add admin console to architecture document" && git push
input.description: Verify branch, commit admin console changes, push

input.command:
gh pr edit 2 --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Adds `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` — the technical architecture for **fold@Scripps**, the companion to `docs/VISION.md`. Where the vision states *why* and *what*, this states *how*. It records the structural decisions, the reasoning behind them, and the boundaries that keep the system simple now while leaving room to grow.

## Key decisions

- **Stack** — FastAPI JSON backend + React/TypeScript/Tailwind SPA (Vite); PostgreSQL; model execution via the [`autobio`](https://github.com/briney/autobio) Python API.
- **Job queue / GPU** — exclusive, variable-count whole-GPU allocation; a custom DB-backed scheduler behind an `Executor` interface (Postgres `SELECT … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, multi-node-reachable). No extra broker.
- **Process model** — hybrid: nginx/API/Postgres in Docker Compose; the scheduler runs as a host systemd process with the Docker + GPU access it needs to launch autobio containers (the API needs neither).
- **Data model** — User (role/tier/status + quota overrides), Tool catalog (schema-synced from autobio, version-pinned), Run (with `hidden_at` soft-delete), Artifact; per-run `inputs/config/outputs` dirs on local FS behind a `Storage` interface.
- **Quotas** — enforce a per-user concurrency cap (tier profile + per-user override) in v1; record GPU-hours from day one so budgets can be switched on later without backfilling.
- **Auth** — local accounts in v1 behind an identity-provider boundary; httpOnly cookie sessions; gated registration (allowlist + admin activation); per-user API tokens as the future programmatic credential.

## Admin console (v1)

Admin-scoped API endpoints + SPA views over the **same service layer** (not a separate system):

- **User management** — registration allowlist/invites, activate/suspend, set tier + per-user quota overrides, admin-initiated password resets, grant/revoke admin (first admin seeded via env/CLI).
- **Job oversight** — view all runs (incl. user-hidden), cancel/kill, hard-delete to reclaim storage.
- **System status** — live 8-GPU pool view, queue depth/waits.
- **Usage reporting** — per-user and system GPU-hours over time.
- **Tool catalog** — trigger autobio sync, enable/disable tools.
- **Maintenance mode** — pause dispatch for node/image maintenance.
- **Audit log** — record admin actions.

## Growth, not walled off

Four swappable boundaries keep each growth axis reachable without building it now:

- **Service layer** (transport-agnostic core) → a programmatic API as another adapter
- **`Executor`** → multi-node / SLURM / k8s
- **`Storage`** → NFS / object storage
- **Identity provider** → SSO (OIDC/SAML) / LDAP

## Non-goals (for now)

Chained pipelines, multi-node scheduling, a public API, hardened auth, GPU packing/sharing, SSE/push status, storage retention/auto-cleanup, finer-grained permissions, and self-service email/password flows — all reachable, none built in v1.
EOF
)"
input.description: Update PR #2 description to include admin console

## Summary

Adds `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` — the technical architecture for **fold@Scripps**, the companion to `docs/VISION.md`. Where the vision states *why* and *what*, this states *how*. It records the structural decisions, the reasoning behind them, and the boundaries that keep the system simple now while leaving room to grow.

## Key decisions

- **Stack** — FastAPI JSON backend + React/TypeScript/Tailwind SPA (Vite); PostgreSQL; model execution via the [`autobio`](https://github.com/briney/autobio) Python API.
- **Job queue / GPU** — exclusive, variable-count whole-GPU allocation; a custom DB-backed scheduler behind an `Executor` interface (Postgres `SELECT … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, multi-node-reachable). No extra broker.
- **Process model** — hybrid: nginx/API/Postgres in Docker Compose; the scheduler runs as a host systemd process with the Docker + GPU access it needs to launch autobio containers (the API needs neither).
- **Data model** — User (role/tier/status + quota overrides), Tool catalog (schema-synced from autobio, version-pinned), Run (with `hidden_at` soft-delete), Artifact; per-run `inputs/config/outputs` dirs on local FS behind a `Storage` interface.
- **Quotas** — enforce a per-user concurrency cap (tier profile + per-user override) in v1; record GPU-hours from day one so budgets can be switched on later without backfilling.
- **Auth** — local accounts in v1 behind an identity-provider boundary; httpOnly cookie sessions; gated registration (allowlist + admin activation); per-user API tokens as the future programmatic credential.

## Admin console (v1)

Admin-scoped API endpoints + SPA views over the **same service layer** (not a separate system):

- **User management** — registration allowlist/invites, activate/suspend, set tier + per-user quota overrides, admin-initiated password resets, grant/revoke admin (first admin seeded via env/CLI).
- **Job oversight** — view all runs (incl. user-hidden), cancel/kill, hard-delete to reclaim storage.
- **System status** — live 8-GPU pool view, queue depth/waits.
- **Usage reporting** — per-user and system GPU-hours over time.
- **Tool catalog** — trigger autobio sync, enable/disable tools.
- **Maintenance mode** — pause dispatch for node/image maintenance.
- **Audit log** — record admin actions.

## Growth, not walled off

Four swappable boundaries keep each growth axis reachable without building it now:

- **Service layer** (transport-agnostic core) → a programmatic API as another adapter
- **`Executor`** → multi-node / SLURM / k8s
- **`Storage`** → NFS / object storage
- **Identity provider** → SSO (OIDC/SAML) / LDAP

## Non-goals (for now)

Chained pipelines, multi-node scheduling, a public API, hardened auth, GPU packing/sharing, SSE/push status, storage retention/auto-cleanup, finer-grained permissions, and self-service email/password flows — all reachable, none built in v1.
EOF

- **Commit:** `814cba5` — "Add admin console to architecture document" (+73 / −6), pushed to `docs/architecture`.
- **PR #2** now reflects the admin console — the description was updated via the REST API (verified the new body is live) after `gh pr edit` tripped over a known Projects-classic deprecation bug.
